decisions.decision: Use Next.js App Router with TypeScript as the application framework
decisions.rationale: Team familiarity, server components for data fetching, excellent Vercel integration

decisions.decision: Embed YouTube clips via IFrame API — never self-host video
decisions.rationale: Zero hosting cost, no copyright risk, YouTube handles CDN and playback
decisions.constraint: Clips must remain publicly available on YouTube; if removed, clip breaks

decisions.decision: File-based shared state for multi-agent coordination (.shared/ directory)
decisions.rationale: Works across separate Claude Code windows without external services, persists between sessions, human-readable

decisions.decision: Use GPT-4o Vision for user recording feedback analysis
decisions.rationale: Best multimodal model for analyzing body language in video frames

decisions.decision: MVP scope: 5 skills × 3 difficulty levels = 15 clips minimum
decisions.rationale: Enough to validate full coaching loop without over-investing in content before product-market fit

decisions.decision: Accept 65 clips as M14 sprint delivery (original target: 100+)
decisions.rationale: Data Engineer hit YouTube Data API v3 daily quota at 65 clips. All 65 are fully verified and complete (script + observation_guide + practice_steps + screenplay_source). 65 clips represents a 4.3× expansion from 15. The remaining 35 clips will be added in the next DE session when quota resets.
alternatives_considered: Block M14 until 100 clips are reached
Use a different YouTube API key to bypass quota
Reduce per-clip data richness to fit more clips in quota

decisions.decision: M16 Learning Materials Builder: Admin creates crawl jobs targeting named communication tactics (beyond 5 standard categories) via YouTube Data API + GPT-4o relevance scoring
decisions.rationale: User requirement: diversify library with niche/advanced communication tactics (Power Pause, Triangle Gaze, Proxemic Management, etc.) that go beyond the standard 5 skill categories. Admin approval gate maintains content quality.

decisions.decision: M21-M25: Platform expansion — arcade admin, analytics dashboards, access control, subscription monetization with PayPal + VNPay
decisions.rationale: User requirement: arcade content management with reference images, user activity analytics, feature performance monitoring, freemium access model (first 3 challenges free), 3-tier subscription (Basic free/Standard/Advanced) with PayPal + VNPay for Vietnam market.

decisions.decision: Fix signal system race conditions: add file locking to signal-send.js, signal-done.js, and create state-update.js helper
decisions.rationale: Multiple roles editing board.json and state files simultaneously caused data loss — M21-M25 milestones were wiped out by concurrent edits. Fix: exclusive file locks (O_EXCL) with stale lock detection + atomic writes (temp+rename).

Three new milestones approved. M26 (Registration Approval) executes first as it blocks M27 and M28. M27 (Discussions) and M28 (AI Assistant) proceed in parallel after M26. Voice platform: OpenAI (Whisper + GPT-4o + TTS) — already a dependency, no new vendor. Comment threading: flat (1 level). Assistant persona: Coach Ney.
alternatives_considered: Separate voice vendor (ElevenLabs) — rejected to avoid new dependency
Sequential M27 then M28 — rejected, they are independent after M26

decisions.decision: M29-M35 Toolkit + Gamification + UX Plan Approved
decisions.rationale: Three initiatives approved: (1) Backend Toolkit with data crawler and embeddable mini-games for data collection, (2) Duolingo-style gamification with XP, streaks, hearts, quests, badges, leagues, leaderboards, (3) UI/UX overhaul with micro-interactions, Coach Ney mascot, bottom tab bar, skill tree. Execution: M29→M30 sequential, M31 parallel with M29, M32 after M31, M33-M35 sequential after M31. 14 new DB models, 21 new API endpoints.
alternatives_considered: Gamification without social features — rejected, leaderboards drive retention
Skip mini-games — rejected, needed for expression data collection
Third-party gamification SDK — rejected, too opinionated for our dark UI

decisions.rationale: Four execution phases: Phase A (parallel, no deps): M36 Error Logging + M37 CMS + M41 Sticky Practice + M42 Submission Review. Phase B (depends M37): M38 Light Mode UI Overhaul. Phase C (depends M37+M38): M39 Homepage Media + M40 App Download. Phase D (depends M25/M26/M30): M43 Tiered Feedback + M44 User Management v2 + M45 Game Data Pipeline. CMS (M37) is the critical path — M38/M39/M40 all depend on it. Error logging (M36) ships independently as infrastructure. M43/M44/M45 extend existing subscription, auth, and mini-game systems.
alternatives_considered: Ship light mode before CMS — rejected, CMS content needed for homepage redesign
Skip error logging — rejected, needed for production debugging
Combine M39+M40 into single milestone — rejected, different content dependencies

decisions.decision: M46 AI Content Generator approved. Free model strategy: Pollinations.ai (no API key, primary) -> Hugging Face free tier (HF_TOKEN, secondary) -> DALL-E 3 (existing OPENAI_API_KEY, fallback). Video generation optional — pipeline works with images only.
decisions.rationale: Enable AI-generated practice content without relying on YouTube. Pollinations.ai requires no API key and is free. HF free tier provides backup. DALL-E uses existing OpenAI key as last resort. New models AiContentRequest + AiGeneratedAsset. Clip model extended with mediaUrl/mediaType for non-YouTube content. Admin review gate before publishing.
alternatives_considered: DALL-E only — rejected, $0.04/image cost adds up
Stable Diffusion self-hosted — rejected, no GPU infrastructure
Stock photo APIs — rejected, not customizable to specific expressions/poses

decisions.decision: M47-M50 Learning Assistant Engine approved. Architecture: abstract SaaS-ready engine at src/engine/learning-assistant/ with pluggable channels, analyzers, planners. Scheduler: Vercel Cron + DB queue. Channels: in-app (M47), push (M48), email/Resend (M49), WhatsApp/Twilio (M50). Engine designed as portable package for any learning domain.
decisions.rationale: Proactive engagement drives retention. Abstract engine allows reuse across learning domains. Channel progression (in-app → push → email → WhatsApp) allows incremental rollout. Free/low-cost providers chosen (Resend free tier, Twilio pay-per-message).
alternatives_considered: Monolithic notification service — rejected, not reusable
Third-party engagement platform (Customer.io, OneSignal) — rejected, vendor lock-in
All channels in single milestone — rejected, too large, incremental delivery preferred
